passages.text: The 'Great Oxidation Event' (GOE), which occurred approximately 2.4 billion years ago, was a pivotal moment in Earth's history. Before this event, the Earth's atmosphere was largely devoid of oxygen, consisting mainly of methane and nitrogen. The rise of cyanobacteria, which produced oxygen through photosynthesis, eventually led to a buildup of this gas in the atmosphere. This shift was catastrophic for many anaerobic organisms, for whom oxygen was toxic, leading to one of the first mass extinctions. However, it also paved the way for the development of multicellular life and the diverse ecosystems we see today. Scientists study mineral deposits from this era to understand the precise timing and causes of this atmospheric transformation.

The Industrial Revolution, which began in Great Britain in the late 18th century, signaled a profound shift in human society. For millennia, human productivity was limited by the physical strength of animals and people. The introduction of steam power and mechanization allowed for a level of production previously unimaginable. This era saw the rise of the factory system, which concentrated labor in urban centers and led to the rapid growth of cities. While it brought about unprecedented economic growth and technological advancement, it also created significant social challenges. Laborers often worked long hours in hazardous conditions, and the lack of urban planning led to squalid living environments. Despite these drawbacks, the Industrial Revolution laid the groundwork for the modern technological age.

passages.title: Cloze Test 17-21
passages.text: Hardly had the government implemented the new tax policy when the public started protesting in the streets.

passages.title: Cloze Test 22-26
passages.text: Doctor: Your blood pressure is still quite high. Are you following the diet we discussed? Patient: I've tried, but it's very difficult to avoid salt when eating out. Doctor: ____

questions.question: The rapid ____ of urban areas has led to significant environmental degradation, necessitating urgent sustainable development policies.

options.A: proliferation
options.B: diminution
options.C: stagnation
options.D: recession
options.E: alleviation

questions.question: Despite extensive research, the underlying mechanism of the neurological disorder remains ____, leaving clinicians without a definitive cure.

options.A: transparent
options.B: elusive
options.C: conspicuous
options.D: tangible
options.E: ubiquitous

questions.question: The philosopher's latest treatise was criticized for being overly ____, making it inaccessible to anyone without a deep background in metaphysics.

options.A: superficial
options.B: esoteric
options.C: lucid
options.D: concise
options.E: redundant

questions.question: ____ the evidence supporting anthropogenic climate change is overwhelming, some skeptics continue to challenge the validity of global warming models.

options.A: Because
options.B: Since
options.C: Although
options.D: In case
options.E: Provided that

questions.question: Genetic engineering holds the potential to eradicate hereditary diseases; ____, it raises profound ethical concerns regarding the manipulation of human DNA.

options.A: furthermore
options.B: consequently
options.C: similarly
options.D: nevertheless
options.E: for instance

questions.question: Economists argue that a nation's prosperity depends not only on its natural resources ____ on its ability to innovate and adapt to global market trends.

options.A: but also
options.B: as well as
options.C: rather than
options.D: or else
options.E: so as

questions.question: Archaeologists discovered a series of ancient manuscripts ____ hidden in a remote cave for over two millennia.

options.A: that had been
options.B: having being
options.C: they were
options.D: which have
options.E: to be

questions.question: The Industrial Revolution (5) ____ the course of human history by shifting the economic base from agriculture to manufacturing.

options.A: altered
options.B: sustained
options.C: neglected
options.D: preceded
options.E: simulated

questions.question: This transition was (6) ____ by the invention of the steam engine, which allowed for mass production.

options.A: impeded
options.B: facilitated
options.C: deterred
options.D: abandoned
options.E: restricted

questions.question: (7) ____, the growth of factories led to a massive migration of workers from rural areas to burgeoning cities.

options.A: However
options.B: Consequently
options.C: Conversely
options.D: Otherwise
options.E: On the contrary

questions.question: Living conditions in these early industrial centers were often (8) ____, characterized by overcrowding and poor sanitation.

options.A: opulent
options.B: deplorable
options.C: exquisite
options.D: prosperous
options.E: superfluous

questions.question: Governments eventually (9) ____ labor laws to protect workers and improve public health standards.

options.A: repealed
options.B: enacted
options.C: boycotted
options.D: ignored
options.E: postponed

questions.question: Sanayi Devrimi sonrasında ortaya çıkan teknolojik gelişmeler, modern toplumun ekonomik ve sosyal yapısını temelden sarsmıştır.

options.A: Technological developments following the Industrial Revolution fundamentally shook the economic and social structure of modern society.
options.B: The Industrial Revolution caused technological developments that supported the economic and social structure of modern society.
options.C: Modern society's economic and social structures were built upon the technological developments of the Industrial Revolution.
options.D: Fundamental changes in the social and economic structure of society were triggered by the Industrial Revolution's technologies.
options.E: After the Industrial Revolution, technology and society evolved together to form a modern economic structure.

questions.question: Many historians argue that the collapse of the Roman Empire was not an abrupt event but a gradual process of decay.

options.A: Birçok tarihçi Roma İmparatorluğu'nun çöküşünün ani bir olay değil, kademeli bir çürüme süreci olduğunu savunmaktadır.
options.B: Tarihçilerin çoğu Roma İmparatorluğu'nun çöküşünü ani bir olaydan ziyade yavaş bir ilerleme olarak görür.
options.C: Roma İmparatorluğu'nun çöküşünün kademeli bir süreç olduğu iddiası birçok tarihçi tarafından kabul edilmektedir.
options.D: Roma İmparatorluğu aniden çökmemiş, aksine tarihçilere göre uzun bir çürüme döneminden geçmiştir.
options.E: Çoğu tarihçiye göre Roma İmparatorluğu'nun çöküşü aniden değil, zamanla yayılan bir gerileme ile olmuştur.

questions.question: Yapay zeka sistemleri karmaşık problemleri çözmede insan zekasını geride bıraksa da, etik karar verme konusunda hala yetersiz kalmaktadır.

options.A: While AI systems surpass human intelligence in solving complex problems, they still fall short in ethical decision-making.
options.B: AI systems are superior to humans in problem solving, yet they are completely incapable of making ethical decisions.
options.C: Despite solving complex problems better than humans, AI systems cannot make any ethical decisions.
options.D: Even though human intelligence is slower at solving problems, it remains superior to AI in ethical matters.
options.E: AI systems are designed to solve complex problems, but they often ignore the ethical dimensions of these problems.

questions.question: The preservation of biodiversity is essential for maintaining the ecological balance of our planet.

options.A: Biyoçeşitliliğin korunması, gezegenimizin ekolojik dengesini sürdürmek için esastır.
options.B: Gezegenimizdeki ekolojik dengenin korunması ancak biyoçeşitlilik ile mümkündür.
options.C: Ekolojik dengeyi korumak adına biyoçeşitliliğin sürdürülmesi zorunludur.
options.D: Biyoçeşitlilik korunmazsa, gezegenimizin ekolojik dengesi bozulacaktır.
options.E: Gezegenimizin temel ihtiyacı olan ekolojik denge, biyoçeşitliliğin korunmasına bağlıdır.

questions.question: Boşluk 17 için en uygun seçeneği bulun.

options.A: As soon as the government introduced the new tax policy, public demonstrations began.
options.B: The public had been protesting for a long time before the government finally changed the tax policy.
options.C: The government decided to implement the tax policy despite the ongoing public protests.
options.D: If the government had not implemented the tax policy, the public would not have protested.
options.E: The public protests forced the government to implement a new tax policy immediately.

questions.question: Boşluk 18 için en uygun seçeneği bulun.

options.A: The rescue team arrived too late to save the climbers from the blizzard.
options.B: The climbers died in the blizzard because the rescue team failed to intervene.
options.C: The rescue team saved the climbers' lives by intervening just in time during the blizzard.
options.D: Even though the rescue team intervened, they could not prevent the climbers from perishing.
options.E: The climbers managed to survive the blizzard without any help from the rescue team.

questions.question: Boşluk 19 için en uygun seçeneği bulun.

options.A: The researchers repeated the experiment because the initial results were not clear.
options.B: Because the experiment was successful, the researchers decided to conduct it one more time.
options.C: The researchers found the results so interesting that they wanted to see them again.
options.D: Despite the clarity of the results, the researchers felt the need to redo the experiment.
options.E: The ambiguity of the results prevented the researchers from ever finishing the experiment.

questions.question: Boşluk 20 için en uygun seçeneği bulun.

options.A: Environmental stress and internal warfare are commonly thought to be the reasons for the Maya civilization's fall.
options.B: The Maya civilization survived environmental stress but was eventually destroyed by internal warfare.
options.C: Many people think that the Maya civilization would have survived if not for environmental stress.
options.D: Internal warfare was the primary reason for the fall of the Maya, regardless of the environment.
options.E: The collapse of the Maya civilization led to severe environmental stress and internal warfare.

questions.question: Boşluk 21 için en uygun seçeneği bulun.

options.A: I have been struggling to manage my time between my part-time job and my studies.
options.B: I think the lab reports are getting easier, so I haven't been putting in much effort.
options.C: The lab equipment is outdated, which makes it difficult to get accurate results.
options.D: I have already submitted all my reports on time this semester.
options.E: I actually find the course material very engaging and easy to follow.

questions.question: Boşluk 22 için en uygun seçeneği bulun.

options.A: Salt is not actually that bad for you if you drink plenty of water.
options.B: You should try preparing your meals at home more often to control your intake.
options.C: Maybe you should stop exercising until your blood pressure goes down.
options.D: It's okay to indulge occasionally as long as you take your medication.
options.E: Eating out is fine, just make sure you order more desserts instead of appetizers.

questions.question: Boşluk 23 için en uygun seçeneği bulun.

options.A: We have implemented the highest standards of encryption to ensure user data remains secure.
options.B: Privacy is not a priority for our company as we focus on profit margins.
options.C: The critics are right, and we are planning to shut down the project immediately.
options.D: Most users don't actually care about their privacy as much as you think.
options.E: We don't collect any data at all, so the critics must be mistaken.

questions.question: Boşluk 24 için en uygun seçeneği bulun.

options.A: So, if I spend time studying, the opportunity cost is the fun I could have had elsewhere?
options.B: Does it mean that every choice we make has the same value?
options.C: Is it related to the total amount of money a person earns in a year?
options.D: So, opportunity cost only applies to large business investments?
options.E: I still don't understand how it differs from the actual cost of a product.

questions.question: Boşluk 25 için en uygun seçeneği bulun.

options.A: However, the initial cost of installing solar panels can be quite high for homeowners.
options.B: Therefore, many countries are investing heavily in infrastructure to support green energy.
options.C: Nuclear power remains a controversial topic among environmentalists worldwide.
options.D: Coal mining continues to be a major source of employment in certain regions.
options.E: The efficiency of wind turbines depends largely on the geographical location.

questions.question: Boşluk 26 için en uygun seçeneği bulun.

options.A: Behaviorism, on the other hand, focuses solely on observable behaviors.
options.B: The human brain is a complex organ that controls all bodily functions.
options.C: In essence, it seeks to understand the 'software' of the human mind.
options.D: Many psychological disorders are treated with a combination of therapy and medication.
options.E: Social psychology explores how individuals are influenced by the presence of others.

questions.question: The Silk Road was not a single path but a network of trade routes connecting the East and the West. It facilitated the exchange of goods like silk, spices, and precious metals. ____. For instance, Buddhism spread from India to China through these ancient corridors.

options.A: The route was often dangerous due to the presence of bandits and harsh terrain.
options.B: Maritime trade eventually replaced the Silk Road as the primary means of commerce.
options.C: Equally important was the transmission of ideas, religions, and technologies.
options.D: The Great Wall of China was built to protect the empire from northern invasions.
options.E: Many merchants became incredibly wealthy by trading along these routes.

questions.question: Artificial Intelligence (AI) is transforming the healthcare industry by enhancing diagnostic accuracy and personalizing treatment plans. Algorithms can analyze medical images with a precision that often exceeds human capabilities. ____. Ultimately, the goal is to augment human expertise rather than replace it.

options.A: However, the use of AI also raises significant concerns regarding data privacy and bias.
options.B: Medical students are now required to take courses in computer programming.
options.C: Robotic surgery is becoming the standard of care for many complex procedures.
options.D: Patients are generally skeptical of diagnoses made by a computer program.
options.E: Pharmaceutical companies are using AI to speed up the drug discovery process.

questions.question: The manager's decision to cut costs was met with fierce resistance from the staff, who feared job losses.

options.A: The staff strongly opposed the manager's cost-cutting measures because they were worried about losing their jobs.
options.B: The manager decided to fire some staff members in order to reduce the company's expenses.
options.C: Despite the staff's protests, the manager went ahead with the plan to reduce the workforce.
options.D: The staff's fear of losing their jobs led the manager to reconsider his decision to cut costs.
options.E: Cost-cutting was necessary, but the staff resisted because they didn't understand the financial situation.

questions.question: Rarely do we encounter a scientific discovery that completely changes our understanding of the universe.

options.A: It is very uncommon to find a scientific breakthrough that fundamentally alters our view of the cosmos.
options.B: Scientific discoveries often change how we perceive the world around us.
options.C: We are used to scientific discoveries that slightly improve our understanding of physics.
options.D: A complete change in our understanding of the universe is something scientists expect every day.
options.E: Most scientific discoveries are revolutionary and change everything we know about the universe.

questions.question: Had the witnesses been more observant, the police might have caught the suspect much sooner.

options.A: The police caught the suspect quickly because the witnesses were very observant.
options.B: If the witnesses had paid more attention, the suspect could have been captured earlier.
options.C: The witnesses were not observant enough, so the police never caught the suspect.
options.D: The suspect was caught sooner than expected, despite the witnesses' lack of observation.
options.E: The police required the witnesses to be more observant in order to identify the suspect.

questions.question: (I) Digital currency, such as Bitcoin, has gained significant popularity over the last decade. (II) Traditional banking systems are increasingly incorporating blockchain technology to improve security. (III) Many investors see it as a volatile yet potentially lucrative asset. (IV) However, its lack of regulation remains a major concern for financial authorities. (V) Governments are now exploring the possibility of creating their own central bank digital currencies.

options.A: I
options.B: II
options.C: III
options.D: IV
options.E: V

questions.question: (I) The Great Barrier Reef is the world's largest coral reef system, located off the coast of Australia. (II) It is home to thousands of species of marine life, including fish, whales, and turtles. (III) Tourism in Australia contributes significantly to the national economy every year. (IV) Unfortunately, rising ocean temperatures are causing widespread coral bleaching. (V) Conservation efforts are underway to protect this fragile ecosystem from further damage.

options.A: I
options.B: II
options.C: III
options.D: IV
options.E: V

questions.question: (I) Shakespeare's plays are known for their complex characters and universal themes. (II) His works have been translated into every major living language and are performed worldwide. (III) The Globe Theatre in London was the original venue for many of his premieres. (IV) Modern adaptations often place his stories in contemporary settings to attract younger audiences. (V) Learning a second language has been shown to improve cognitive function in older adults.

options.A: I
options.B: II
options.C: III
options.D: IV
options.E: V

questions.question: According to the passage, why is the 'Great Oxidation Event' considered a turning point in Earth's history?

options.A: It led to the extinction of all anaerobic life forms.
options.B: It provided the oxygen necessary for the evolution of complex life.
options.C: It marked the moment when the first oceans were formed.
options.D: It was the first time that volcanic activity ceased on Earth.
options.E: It allowed plants to move from the sea to the land.

questions.question: What was the primary cause of the increase in atmospheric oxygen during the GOE?

options.A: Massive volcanic eruptions releasing oxygen.
options.B: The breakdown of methane by solar radiation.
options.C: The photosynthetic activity of cyanobacteria.
options.D: A sudden change in the Earth's orbital path.
options.E: The cooling of the Earth's core.

questions.question: It can be inferred from the passage that anaerobic organisms ____.

options.A: thrived in the new oxygen-rich atmosphere
options.B: were responsible for the GOE themselves
options.C: existed before oxygen became abundant in the atmosphere
options.D: are the ancestors of all modern multicellular life
options.E: are no longer found anywhere on Earth today

questions.question: What is the main idea of the passage regarding the Industrial Revolution?

options.A: It was solely responsible for modern environmental problems.
options.B: It transformed the social and economic landscape of the world.
options.C: It began in the United States before spreading to Europe.
options.D: It focused primarily on improving agricultural techniques.
options.E: It led to a decrease in the global population due to poor conditions.

questions.question: According to the passage, how did production change during the Industrial Revolution?

options.A: It became more dependent on animal power.
options.B: It was moved from factories back to small home workshops.
options.C: It reached levels that were never possible before mechanization.
options.D: It focused on quality over quantity for the first time.
options.E: It was restricted by new government regulations.

questions.question: The author mentions 'hazardous conditions' and 'squalid living environments' to ____.

options.A: argue that the Industrial Revolution was a mistake
options.B: highlight the negative social impacts of the era
options.C: suggest that people were happier before the 18th century
options.D: explain why the factory system eventually failed
options.E: show that urban planning was highly developed at the time
